content.full_text:
DAVID BENGSTON
AGRICULTURAL COMMISSIONER
DIRECTOR OF WEIGHTS
AND MEASURES

OFFICE LOCATION
AGRICULTURAL CENTER
579 LOW GAP ROAD

TELEPHONE (707) 463-4208
FAX (707) 463-0240

COUNTY OF MENDOCINO
DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE
COURTHOUSE
UKIAH, CALIFORNIA 95482

JUNE 1, 1992

Fair Political Practices Committee
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento CA 95804
Attention: John Wallace

Dear John,

I talked to you on the telephone last week regarding a land use
planning referral that I have received and a criminal matter in
which I am testifying. You said that you did not see any
conflict, but you thought it would be a good idea if I wrote you
a letter and received a written opinion.

The background of why I called you in the first place was upon a
recommendation from our attorney at County Counsel, Yves Herbert,
who did not think that I had a conflict, but suggested that I
call your office.

The criminal matter involves an action by the DA for the State of
California against Omni Foundation. They are being held
accountable for a toxic waste fire on their property. I do not
have a list of the actual charges. I was subpoenaed (see
attached) in November, and I testified shortly thereafter in the
pre-trial. The matter is going to trial in August. I testified
as an expert witness as to the type of fire that it was, that is,
that it was pesticide related.

The land use planning matter is a proposal for a residential sub-
division on the same piece of property as the fire which is owned
by Omni Foundation. My opinion in this matter (see attached) is
that the project is premature, ill-founded, and would be very
detrimental to agriculture. I have no investment in any of the
companies or properties mentioned.

My question to you is do I have any potential conflicts of which I
need to be aware? I have checked with both the County Counsel
and the District Attorney to see if either matter would
compromise the other matter and they did not think so. I would
appreciate any thoughts that you might have on these matters.

Sincerely,

David Bengston
Agricultural Commissioner

attachments

COUNTY OF MENDOCINO, STATE OF CALIFORNIA RECEIVED

DISTRICT ATTORNEY'S SUBPOENA - CRIMINAL
NOV 2 7 1991

The People of the State of California, To:

COUNTY OF MENDOCINO
DAVID BENGSTON Department of Agriculture
COUNTY AGRICULTURE, COMMISSIONER

YOU ARE COMMANDED TO APPEAR BEFORE THE JUDGE OF THE BELOW DESIGNATED
COURT, ON THE DATE AND TIME INDICATED, AS A WITNESS IN A CRIMINAL ACTION
PROSECUTED BY THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA.

AGAINST: OMNI FOUNDATION
DA NO: F-91-18029-1 AGENCY & NO: MCHD CRIME: 25189.7 HS

COURT: MUNICIPAL COURT OF THE MT SANHEDRIN COURT #: C-6180
COURTHOUSE DEPT. 4
UKIAH, CA 95482 Floor 2

CRIMINAL ACTION: PRELIM HEARING
DATE: JANUARY 23, 1992
TIME: 9:00 A.M.

AND YOU ARE REQUIRED TO BRING WITH YOU THE FOLLOWING:

*NOTICE TO PERSON BEING SERVED:

CONTACT the District Attorney's Office (707)463-4211 THE DAY BEFORE above date TO
VERIFY that this case will go to court as scheduled.

RESCHEDULES/CONTINUANCES Trial as rescheduled or continued at times, and some
require witnesses to be present on different days and times. If the case has been
rescheduled, you need not appear if you contact the District Attorney's Office by phone
and agree to appear at a new date and/or time, in which case you MUST appear as agreed
by phone. If you do not wish to accept this change of date/time, you then MUST appear
as stated on this subpoena, at which time the court will order your return for the new
date and time. (Section 1331.5 Penal Code)

FAILURE TO APPEAR in response to this subpoena at the time and place set forth
above, or another time agreed upon between you and the undersigned, would subject you to
arrest and punishment for contempt of court. (Sections 166 & 1331 Penal Code, 109 CCP)

DATED: November 25, 1991 SUSAN MASSINI
DISTRICT ATTORNEY

[signature]
Deputy District Attorney

NOTICE: You may be entitled to witness fees, mileage, or both, at
the discretion of the Court. To make such request, after your appearance
contact the Victim/Witness Assistance Office.(1329.1 PC)

DAVID BENGSTON
AGRICULTURAL COMMISSIONER
DIRECTOR OF WEIGHTS
AND MEASURES

OFFICE LOCATION
AGRICULTURAL CENTER
579 LOW GAP ROAD

TELEPHONE (707) 463-4208
FAX (707) 463-0240

COUNTY OF MENDOCINO
DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE
COURTHOUSE
UKIAH, CALIFORNIA 95482

May 26, 1992

TO: Pam Townsend, Planning

FROM: David Bengston, Ag.

SUBJECT: OMNI FOUNDATION GENERAL PLAN AMENDMENT AND REZONE

First, for the record, let me state that I am currently
testifying in a criminal matter in which Omni Corporation is the
defendant. I was subpoenaed to testify as to a type of fire, ie.
that it was pesticide related. It has nothing to do with our
department's views on the proposed project.

The office of the Agricultural Commissioner is completely opposed
to this proposed project. The property in question is surrounded
by extremely good viable vineyards that are currently producing
good crops. The vineyards that would be adversely affected by
this project include: Bruguci's, Tollini's, Ventini's, Morals,
Ricetti's, and Mendocino Vineyards'. These are all premium
winegrape vineyards. Most of these growers expect to stay in
the business of farming and they are opposed to this type of
project. One of the growers quoted said, "Don't let them take
away our upland bench". Two of the growers called it the "best
grapeland in Mendocino County" and another grower said it was
"real good grapelands with good gravel soils and good drainage".

In my opinion, this project is premature and would ultimately
drive agriculture out of the area. The high density of housing,
traffic and people would have a very negative impact on the
vineyards in the area. It is ridiculous to go from Ag 40 all the
way down to 6000 square foot lots in this area. The growers
would not be able to carry on normal agricultural practices
without complaints.

This is contrary to the General Plan: Goal #1 of protecting and
maintaining prime agricultural land; Policy 1a.- encroaching upon
it with incompatible uses; Policy 1c.- prohibiting new non-
agricultural uses in agricultural areas which interfere with
normal operations; 1h.-locating new nonagricultural uses on prime
agricultural lands; Goal #2 of minimizing the conflicts between
agricultural operations and other land uses; and 2a.- limiting
residential uses and subdivisions adjacent to Type I Ag Preserves
to a low density standard.

PRIORITY FOR THIS APPLICATION IS REQUESTED PURSUANT TO
GOAL 4, POLICY 4.3 OF POLICIES AND IMPLEMENTATION OF THE
MENDOCINO COUNTY GENERAL PLAN
